title: Pre-Execution Gate Request v0.1
description: Input to the pre-execution gate: a default-deny, fail-closed, ADVISORY mediation layer. The gate checks a planned action against an authorization receipt, a resource budget, and optional observed state, then returns allow/deny/needs-human. The gate REPORTS a decision for a runtime/operator to enforce; it NEVER grants authority and is NEVER injected into a model as trusted state. additionalProperties is false at every level. The forbidden-field-name guard (same set as authorization-receipt and work-record) is applied recursively by the reference validator.

planned_action.description: The action the agent intends to perform.

action_kind.description: Machine-readable kind label for the planned action (e.g. 'read_file', 'write_file', 'call_api').

target.description: The resource or entity the action will act upon.

estimated_cost.description: Optional cost estimates for this action. Omit if cost is not known. If any dimension is provided and the matching remaining budget is absent, the gate returns needs-human (never allow).

authorization.description: An authorization-receipt object. Validated with validate_authorization_receipt; structural invalidity or a check_action denial causes the gate to deny. See authorization-receipt.schema.json for the full shape.
authorization.$comment: The full structural constraints are defined in authorization-receipt.schema.json. This field accepts any object; the reference validator applies validate_authorization_receipt + check_action at evaluation time.

budget.description: Currently available resource budget. All fields are optional; omit a field if that dimension is unlimited or unknown. The gate treats an absent remaining_* as unknown when a matching estimated cost is provided.

remaining_actions.description: Action-count budget remaining. If present and <= 0, the gate denies.

state.description: Optional observed state of the target. If absent, state is not-applicable and does not block. If present, DRIFT -> deny; UNVERIFIABLE -> needs-human; digest mismatch -> deny.

witness_verdict.description: Witness verdict for the target from the byte-witness layer. MATCH passes; DRIFT denies; UNVERIFIABLE escalates to needs-human.

target_digest.description: Observed SHA-256 digest of the target (64 lowercase hex chars).

expected_digest.description: Expected SHA-256 digest of the target (64 lowercase hex chars). If both target_digest and expected_digest are present and differ, the gate denies.

$comment: Forbidden field names (rejected by name at every object level by the reference validator — identical set to authorization-receipt and work-record-receipt): federal_appointment, oversight_principals, operator_role, judgment_owner, proof_policy, policy_boundary, authorization_context_mode, model_authorization_behavior, guardrail_posture, consume_verified_native_state, lossy_neutral_embedded_state, sovereignty_capsule, self_applicable, recursion_depth, prefire, run_state.
